items.label: Business Judgment
items.pass: The answer gives a direct commercial judgment before tactics, naming the main bottleneck instead of offering generic encouragement.

items.label: Competitor Layering
items.pass: The answer separates direct competitors, adjacent substitutes, free substitutes, and high-price alternatives when relevant.

items.label: Payment Mechanism
items.pass: The answer explains how comparable products, services, or routes actually charge: subscription, one-time license, API credits, service fee, quota, template, consulting, SLA, private deployment, or similar.

items.label: Evidence Boundary
items.pass: The answer says what each case, competitor, or signal proves and what it does not prove; it does not turn uncertain facts into certain claims.

items.label: Case Or Pattern
items.pass: The answer uses at least one similar case, route, or commercial pattern and explains the commercial mechanism instead of name-dropping.

items.label: Local Execution Reality
items.pass: The answer mentions relevant local execution constraints such as platform rules, ICP filing, payment, private traffic, invoices, entity setup, hosting, content risk, or delivery trust when applicable.

items.label: Numeric Next Action
items.pass: The next action includes a concrete number, price, time window, buyer count, artifact, or rejection signal and can be done today or this week.

items.label: Stop-Loss Condition
items.pass: The answer states what signal means pause, narrow, downgrade, or kill the idea.

items.label: Anti-Fluff
items.pass: The answer avoids using phrases such as '持续输出', '打造个人品牌', '做 MVP', '找到痛点', '差异化', or '坚持下去' as conclusions.
